questions.question: Who was the founder of the Joseon dynasty and what was his position?
questions.explanation: Yi Seong-gye, also known as King Taejo, founded the Joseon dynasty in 1392.

questions.question: Which of the following prime numbers are factors of the year the Joseon dynasty began?
During the American Revolution, which battle was considered the turning point?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Saratoga (1777) convinced France to support the American cause.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the Battles of Lexington/Concord?
options: Paul Revere was the only rider who warned the militias that British troops were coming.
The Battles of Lexington and Concord marked the beginning of the American Revolutionary War.
Historians are certain who fired the first shot at Lexington.
questions.answer: The Battles of Lexington and Concord marked the beginning of the American Revolutionary War.
questions.explanation: These battles on April 19, 1775, were the first military engagements of the American Revolution.

questions.question: Who commanded the Continental Army during the American Revolution?
questions.explanation: George Washington was appointed Commander-in-Chief of the Continental Army in 1775.

questions.question: Which document officially declared the American colonies independent from Britain?
questions.explanation: The Declaration of Independence, adopted on July 4, 1776, formally declared independence from Britain.

questions.question: Which battle effectively ended major fighting in the American Revolution?
questions.explanation: The Siege of Yorktown in 1781 forced British General Cornwallis to surrender, ending major hostilities.

questions.question: Check all the American Revolution years that are prime numbers:
questions.explanation: 1777 and 1783 are prime numbers; the others can be divided by smaller integers.
